focus: traceability — metadata projections, benchmark gates, and phase acceptance

findingDetails.title: Baseline benchmark passes seven route-contract violations
findingDetails.evidence: Report verdict is PASS, hubRoute.regressions is zero, and all 13 scenarios are labeled passed while routeGoldRows is zero. Telemetry records MT-004 selecting six modes and MT-003 plus five holdouts selecting no mode.
findingDetails.impact: The deterministic CI signal certifies broken routing and can release the hub without detecting its positive, defer, or holdout failures.
findingDetails.recommendation: Treat as a separate benchmark-schema/gate remediation; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Verdict, gate counters, route-gold counters, all 13 rows, and scenario frontmatter expectations were compared.

findingDetails.title: Derived graph intent projection omits three added modes
findingDetails.evidence: Root intent_signals and derived entities cover all six modes, but derived.intent_signals lists only Chrome, ClickUp, and Figma vocabulary. Edge contexts and causal summary likewise narrate only three modes.
findingDetails.impact: Graph consumers receive inconsistent routing inventories depending on which projection they read, reducing discovery for Aside, Refero, and Mobbin.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reconcile through a separate metadata regeneration/remediation; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All root and derived inventory fields plus outward-edge summaries were compared inside the single graph metadata document.

findingDetails.title: Phase acceptance package remains scoped to three modes
findingDetails.evidence: Spec, plan, and tasks repeatedly define a three-mode matrix and do not name Aside, Refero, or Mobbin. The execution note says only a .gitkeep baseline exists although a six-mode baseline report is committed.
findingDetails.impact: The active target cannot demonstrate requirement-to-scenario or task-to-evidence coverage for half of the current hub inventory.
findingDetails.recommendation: Use a separate planning/spec amendment; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Requirements, success criteria, plan axes, tasks, status, execution note, and benchmark paths were compared.

findingDetails.title: Hub playbook index omits three modes and nine scenarios
findingDetails.evidence: The index describes only Chrome, ClickUp, Figma and a four-scenario table, while the directory contains 13 gold files covering Aside, Refero, Mobbin and six holdouts.
findingDetails.impact: Operators using the documented directory under-test the expanded hub and miss its generalization corpus.
findingDetails.recommendation: Refresh through a separate documentation task; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Index table and related links were compared to the complete hub_routing file inventory.

results.reason: Requirements and success criteria cover three modes while code exposes six.
Tasks are unchecked and committed evidence passes unscored route expectations.
Registry-router keys and aliases align, but the graph derived projection does not.
Index omits expanded surface and benchmark has zero route-gold rows.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: A six-mode hub is projected through registry, router, graph, benchmark, playbook, and phase acceptance consumers.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These are the producer and downstream projections that claim inventory and acceptance coverage.

searchLedger.invariant: A routing scenario cannot pass when observed workflowMode violates expected_intent.

searchActions.queryOrPath: 13 report telemetry rows versus scenario expected_intent
searchActions.result: Seven mismatches are labeled passed while routeGoldRows equals zero.

searchLedger.rationale: The designated deterministic gate does not consume route gold.

searchLedger.invariant: Every published graph projection represents the same six-mode inventory.

searchActions.result: Derived intent signals and narrative edges preserve the original three-mode set.

searchLedger.rationale: The single metadata file exposes contradictory inventories.

searchLedger.invariant: The active acceptance packet covers every current router mode and evidence path.

searchActions.result: No added-mode requirement or task exists; all benchmark axes say three modes.

searchLedger.rationale: Half the current inventory has no requirement-to-evidence chain.

searchLedger.invariant: The operator index enumerates the committed routing corpus.

searchActions.queryOrPath: playbook index versus hub_routing file inventory
searchActions.result: Index lists 4 of 13 scenarios and 3 of 6 modes.

searchLedger.rationale: The documented validation entry point hides the expanded corpus.

title: Baseline benchmark passes seven route-contract violations

reason: All six keys align and every registry alias is reachable through its mode's router classes.
